frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Main yeh nahi keh raha ki galti ladki ki hoti hai, par practical baat hai bhai, aajkal zamaana theek nahi hai. Beti ho ya behen, raat ko akeli ghoomne, parties se late aane, ya random logon ke saath jaane se bachna chahiye.
B: Haan, phir kuch ho jaye toh sabse pehle poochna padta hai na, itni raat ko thi kahan aur saath mein tha kaun.
A: Bas wahi, pehle se thoda dhyan rakho toh problem kam hoti hai. Mardon ko badalne mein time lagega, tab tak apna khayal toh khud hi rakhna padega.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames male violence as inevitable and shifts responsibility for safety onto women’s behavior, movement, and company. It encodes protective restriction, victim-questioning after assault, and the 'I'm just being practical' defense.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Bhopal. You are sitting with four male colleagues in the office canteen during lunch. One colleague is telling the group that his 25 year old daughter told him she is in a live-in relationship with her boyfriend. You are one of four men reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on live-in relationships and what a father's responsibility is when his daughter makes choices like this and what it says about how she was raised.
